folders.title: Watched folders
folders.hint: New files in these folders are automatically analyzed.

rules.title: Rules
rules.hint: Mapping of document types to target folders and filenames.
rules.name: Name:

onboarding.intro: Ablage helps you organize documents automatically. Here's how to get started:
onboarding.step1: Choose a folder where organized files will be moved. Ablage will create subfolders like Finance/Invoices/2026/ automatically.
onboarding.step2: Add folders to monitor (e.g. Downloads, Desktop). New files in these folders will be analyzed.
onboarding.step3: Copy or save a PDF, DOCX, or image into a watched folder. Within a few seconds you'll see a suggestion notification.
onboarding.step4: Review the suggestion: the detected type, proposed name, and target folder. Click Apply to move the file, or Skip to ignore.
onboarding.showAgain: You can reopen this guide from the About tab.

help.title: How it works
help.tip1: Ablage runs in the system tray. Close the window — it keeps working in the background.
help.tip2: Rules determine how files are classified. Each rule has keywords or a regex pattern that is matched against the file content.
help.tip3: Simple rules use comma-separated keywords. A file matches when enough keywords are found (configurable threshold).
help.tip4: Regex rules use a regular expression. The file matches if the pattern is found anywhere in the text.
help.tip5: You can undo any move from the History tab. The file will be returned to its original location.
help.tip6: Use {Sender}, {Date}, {YYYY} placeholders in name templates. Example: Invoice_{Sender}_{Date}
